commands.review_summary:
This PR threads Bedrock `aws-sdk` request-auth provenance through `ModelRegistry.getApiKeyAndHeaders()`, updates the compaction safeguard to accept that mode without static request credentials, and adds focused regression coverage.

PR surface: Source -6, Tests +41. Total +35 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main can represent Bedrock aws-sdk auth as successful without static key/header material, and the compaction safeguard then cancels that result. I did not run a live AWS/Bedrock reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed because the PR has no actionable review findings and is ready for normal maintainer/CI handling.

target.title: Fix Bedrock aws-sdk compaction auth
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix compaction safeguard request-auth handling so configured `aws-sdk` auth can proceed without static API keys or request headers.
- Thread the Bedrock `aws-sdk` auth mode from `ModelRegistry.getApiKeyAndHeaders()` into the safeguard decision instead of treating the successful lookup as missing credentials.
- Add regression coverage for the compaction handler and registry auth result. Scope is request-auth provenance only; no provider config/default changes and no Bedrock request signing changes.

Fixes #90179

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Compaction must continue for Bedrock models authenticated through AWS SDK session/profile credentials even when there is no static `apiKey` or request header material. Latest `origin/main` still cancels the production compaction-safeguard handler for a successful `{ ok: true, authMode: "aws-sdk" }` request-auth lookup; the patched head returns a compaction result for the same handler path.

Real environment tested: Local Linux worktrees at latest `origin/main` (`82e5dd4da74fea7f7a47542d895fd25c85baf07c`) and patched PR head (`18685c13deafde99eb460ab269835c6fee9c9204`). The source-runtime proof imported the real `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts` production handler and real runtime registry, set `runtime.model` to a Bedrock Converse model, and used a successful `aws-sdk` request-auth result with missing `apiKey` and missing `headers`. The summarizer function was replaced with a local deterministic test double so the proof exercises the safeguard/auth gate without calling a live LLM or AWS service.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
MAIN_PROOF_WORKTREE="/home/0668001029/openclaw-worktrees/issue-90179-main-proof"
PATCHED_WORKTREE="/home/0668001029/openclaw-worktrees/issue-90179"
EVIDENCE_DIR="/home/0668001029/openclaw-evidence/openclaw-issue-90179-evidence"

env -C "$MAIN_PROOF_WORKTREE" OPENCLAW_REPO_ROOT="$MAIN_PROOF_WORKTREE" PROOF_RUN_LABEL="latest-main-82e5dd4da7" EXPECTED_RESULT="cancel" corepack pnpm@11.2.2 exec tsx "$EVIDENCE_DIR/aws-sdk-compaction-proof.ts" > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/aws-sdk-main-output.txt"

env -C "$PATCHED_WORKTREE" OPENCLAW_REPO_ROOT="$PATCHED_WORKTREE" PROOF_RUN_LABEL="patched-head-18685c13de" EXPECTED_RESULT="compaction" corepack pnpm@11.2.2 exec tsx "$EVIDENCE_DIR/aws-sdk-compaction-proof.ts" > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/aws-sdk-patched-output.txt"

env -C "$PATCHED_WORKTREE" node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts src/agents/sessions/model-registry.test.ts --reporter=verbose > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/focused-tests-output.txt"

env -C "$PATCHED_WORKTREE" node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts src/agents/sessions/model-registry.ts src/agents/sessions/model-registry.test.ts > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/oxlint-output.txt"

env -C "$PATCHED_WORKTREE" corepack pnpm@11.2.2 tsgo:core > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/tsgo-core-output.txt"

env -C "$PATCHED_WORKTREE" corepack pnpm@11.2.2 check:test-types > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/check-test-types-output.txt"

git -C "$PATCHED_WORKTREE" diff --check origin/main..HEAD > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/diff-check-output.txt"
```

Evidence after fix:
```text
Latest origin/main (82e5dd4da74fea7f7a47542d895fd25c85baf07c), same source-runtime compaction handler proof:
PROOF_RUN_LABEL=latest-main-82e5dd4da7
REQUEST_AUTH=ok:true authMode:aws-sdk apiKey:<missing> headers:<missing>
RESULT=cancel
RESULT_CANCEL=true
HAS_COMPACTION=false
SUMMARY_CONTAINS_AWS_SDK=false

Patched PR head (18685c13deafde99eb460ab269835c6fee9c9204), same source-runtime compaction handler proof:
PROOF_RUN_LABEL=patched-head-18685c13de
REQUEST_AUTH=ok:true authMode:aws-sdk apiKey:<missing> headers:<missing>
RESULT=compaction
RESULT_CANCEL=false
HAS_COMPACTION=true
SUMMARY_CONTAINS_AWS_SDK=true

Focused regression tests on patched PR head:
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       102 passed (102)

Additional checks on patched PR head:
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ... exited 0
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 tsgo:core exited 0
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 check:test-types exited 0
git diff --check origin/main..HEAD produced no output.
```

Observed result after fix: The same compaction-safeguard handler path that cancels on latest `origin/main` now accepts explicit `authMode: "aws-sdk"` as valid request authentication, reaches the summarization path, and returns a compaction result. Providers without an API key, headers, or `aws-sdk` auth mode still hit the existing missing-credentials safeguard.

What was not tested: No live AWS account, `AWS_PROFILE`, STS session, IAM role, Bedrock endpoint, Telegram delivery, or Docker deployment was used. The proof is source-runtime local verification of the exact safeguard/auth mismatch that ClawSweeper marked source-repro, with the LLM summarizer isolated to avoid external credentials and network calls.

## Regression Test Plan

- `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts` now covers the production handler path where `runtime.model` is a Bedrock Converse model and request auth succeeds with `authMode: "aws-sdk"` but no static key/header material.
- `src/agents/sessions/model-registry.test.ts` now asserts Bedrock `aws-sdk` registry auth returns `authMode: "aws-sdk"` alongside the expected missing `apiKey` and `headers`.
- Focused command run: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts src/agents/sessions/model-registry.test.ts --reporter=verbose`.

## Root Cause

- `ModelRegistry.getApiKeyAndHeaders()` already accepted Bedrock `auth: "aws-sdk"` without static key material, but returned only `{ ok: true, apiKey: undefined, headers: undefined }`.
- `resolveModelAuth()` in `compaction-safeguard.ts` interpreted any successful request-auth lookup without key/header material as missing credentials and cancelled compaction before the existing Bedrock AWS SDK runtime path could be used.
- The fix preserves the registry's auth mode on the request-auth result and lets the safeguard accept only the explicit `aws-sdk` mode while keeping the existing fail-closed behavior for genuinely missing credentials.
target.author: mushuiyu886
